source_file: The corporate social credit system in China and its transnational impact.txt

93
   Charles Lipson, ‘Why are Some International Agreements Informal?’ (1991) 45(4) International Organ-
   ization 500 as referred to by Cutler (n 8) 729.
94
   See Ohlberg, Ahmed and Lang (n 23) 11.
95
   See van Deursen and Kummeling (n 67) 924.
96
   See Mac Sithigh and Siems (n 28) 1050. According to oﬃcial documents, individuals have to be
   informed before they are added on a blacklist and should have the right to appeal. In practice
   however, there have been cases in which individuals were not informed and did not have recourse
   to appeal. See Creemers (n 15) 19.
97
   See Chen and Cheung (n 29) 372.
 308        S. JAKOB
